skill.name: seo-structure-architect
skill.description: Analyzes page structure, header hierarchy, schema opportunities, internal links, and content flow for search-friendly information architecture.
skill.summary: Builds SEO-focused content structure plans with headers, schema suggestions, and internal linking guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives after context review. The backtick detections are Markdown formatting, the weak cryptography alert maps to ordinary description text, and the network reconnaissance alert maps to SEO schema terminology. No evidence found for command execution, network activity, credential access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but line 25 only formats a local documentation path in Markdown. No command is executed and no user input reaches a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap a file path in prose. There is no executable code or shell invocation in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Structure Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but lines 79 through 85 are a fenced example of an SEO heading outline. The block contains no executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is a Markdown code fence used to display an outline. It does not define a script or invoke an external process.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3, but that line is plain skill description text about content headers. No cryptographic API or hash algorithm appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive YAML front matter, not code. No evidence found for cryptographic use in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported network reconnaissance at line 63, but the line lists Organization and LocalBusiness as schema markup types. It does not describe scanning hosts or contacting a network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is SEO structured data vocabulary. No network commands, host discovery steps, or external endpoints are present.

content.value_statement: Pages often lose search visibility when headings, schema, and internal links lack clear structure. This skill turns content drafts into organized SEO blueprints for users and search engines.
actual_capabilities: Reviews H1 through H6 hierarchy for logical order and topic coverage.
Suggests schema types such as Article, FAQ, HowTo, BreadcrumbList, and LocalBusiness.
Maps internal linking opportunities across parent, child, and related content.
Creates table of contents and jump link recommendations for long pages.
Proposes snippet-friendly formats for definitions, lists, steps, and comparisons.
Gives platform-aware notes for WordPress, Astro, static sites, URLs, and sitemaps.
limitations: It does not perform live keyword research or search volume analysis.
It does not crawl websites or verify published markup in search tools.
It provides recommendations, not guaranteed ranking improvements.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the scanned file tree.

use_cases.title: Restructure a Blog Draft
use_cases.description: Convert a rough article into a clear heading hierarchy with schema and snippet opportunities.

use_cases.title: Plan a Topic Cluster
use_cases.description: Create parent, child, and supporting page relationships with internal link priorities.

use_cases.title: Prepare Developer Handoff
use_cases.description: Turn content structure decisions into implementation notes for schema, breadcrumbs, and page navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit One Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single draft or published page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page outline for SEO structure. Check the H1, H2, and H3 hierarchy, then suggest improvements, schema types, and internal links.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Content Blueprint
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a new article or guide.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SEO structure blueprint for a page about [topic]. Include a heading outline, table of contents, schema recommendations, and snippet formats.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Topic Silo
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a topic silo for [main topic]. Define pillar pages, child pages, cross-links, breadcrumb paths, and content gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Technical Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff for writers, developers, and SEO owners.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this SEO content plan into a technical handoff. Include schema choices, URL structure, sitemap priorities, jump links, and implementation notes for [platform].

output_examples.input: A draft guide has one H1, seven unrelated H2 sections, no FAQ section, and no internal links.
output_examples.output: The skill would group the H2 sections into a logical sequence, add relevant H3 subsections, recommend FAQ and Article schema, and identify internal links to supporting guides.

output_examples.input: A service page needs stronger local SEO structure and clearer navigation.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest LocalBusiness, BreadcrumbList, and FAQ schema, a clearer service-focused H1, location-specific sections, and contextual links to related services.

output_examples.input: A site owner wants a content cluster around technical SEO audits.
output_examples.output: The skill would define a pillar page, supporting audit topic pages, parent-child relationships, breadcrumb paths, and internal link priorities.

best_practices: Provide the current outline, target audience, and primary topic before asking for structure changes.
Ask for separate recommendations for headings, schema, internal links, and snippets.
Review suggestions against brand style, technical limits, and real search intent before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to replace keyword research, analytics review, or competitive SERP analysis.
Do not publish schema suggestions without validating them in your own implementation.
Do not force keywords into every heading when it harms readability or intent.

faq.question: What does this skill help optimize?
faq.answer: It helps optimize page structure, heading hierarchy, schema opportunities, internal links, and snippet-ready formatting.

faq.question: Can it write complete articles?
faq.answer: It can guide structure and organization, but it is focused on architecture rather than full article drafting.

faq.question: Does it generate schema markup?
faq.answer: It recommends schema types and can outline implementation needs, but teams should validate final markup before deployment.

faq.question: Is it useful for existing pages?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current headings, page goal, and related URLs so it can propose targeted improvements.

faq.question: Does it require live website access?
faq.answer: No. It works from content, outlines, URLs, or page descriptions that you provide.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
